{{Short description|none}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Guam women's national football team}}
{{Use mdy dates|date=November 2023}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name              = Guam
| Badge             = Guam national soccer team.svg
| Badge_size        = 200px
| Nickname          = ''Matao'' (The Noblemen)<ref>{{cite news |url=http://mvguam.com/sports/23944-guam-mens-national-soccer-team-now-known-as-matao.html |title=Guam men's national soccer team now known as 'Matao' |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140317140849/http://mvguam.com/sports/23944-guam-mens-national-soccer-team-now-known-as-matao.html|author=Mike Nauta Jr. |work=Marianas Variety |location=Guam |date=1 June 2012 |archive-date=17 March 2014|access-date=2 June 2012 }}</ref>
| Association       = [[Guam Football Association]] (GFA)
| Confederation     = [[Asian Football Confederation|AFC]] (Asia)
| Sub-confederation = [[East Asian Football Federation|EAFF]] (East Asia)
| Coach             = [[Ross Awa]]
| Captain           = [[Jason Cunliffe]] 
| Most caps         = [[Jason Cunliffe]] (74)
| Top scorer        = [[Jason Cunliffe]] (26)
| Home Stadium      = [[GFA National Training Center]]
| FIFA Trigramme    = GUM
| FIFA Rank         = {{FIFA World Rankings|GUM}}
| FIFA max          = 146
| FIFA max date     = August–September 2015
| FIFA min          = 207
| FIFA min date     = March 2022
| Elo Rank          = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Guam}}
| Elo max           = 96
| Elo max date      = June 2015
| Elo min           = 108
| Elo min date      = March 2007
<!--Home Shirt-->
| pattern_la1      = _gum23h
| pattern_b1       = _gum23h
| pattern_ra1      = _gum23h
| pattern_sh1      =
| pattern_so1      =
| leftarm1         = 
| body1            = 
| rightarm1        = 
| shorts1          = 110D59
| socks1           =  800000
| pattern_la2      = _gum23a
| pattern_b2       = _gum23a
| pattern_ra2      = _gum23a
| pattern_sh2      =
| pattern_so2      =
| leftarm2         = 
| body2            = 
| rightarm2        = 
| shorts2          = 800000
| socks2           = 800000
| First game   = {{fb|GUM}} 1–5 {{fb-rt|SOL|1956}}<br>([[Tumon, Guam|Tumon]], [[Guam]]; 2 August 1975)
| Largest win  = {{nowrap|''Unofficial''<br>{{fb|PLW}} 2–15 {{fb-rt|GUM}}<br>(Koror City, Palau; 1 August 1998)<br>''Official''<br>{{fb|GUM}} 9–0 {{fb-rt|NMI}}<br>([[Hagåtña]], [[Guam]]; 1 April 2007)}}
| Largest loss = {{fb|PRK}} 21–0 {{fb-rt|GUM}}<br>([[Taipei]], [[Taiwan]]; 11 March 2005)
| American = true
| 2ndRegional name      = [[Football at the Pacific Games|Pacific Games]]
| 2ndRegional cup apps  = 5
| 2ndRegional cup first = [[Football at the 1975 South Pacific Games|1975]]
| 2ndRegional cup best  = Sixth place ([[Football at the 1979 South Pacific Games|1979]])
|3rdRegional cup apps=1|3rdRegional name=[[Micronesian Games]]|3rdRegional cup first=[[Football at the Micronesian Games#1998|1998]]|3rdRegional cup best=Runners-up ([[Football at the Micronesian Games#1998|1998]])
}}

The '''Guam national football team''' represents [[Guam]], an [[overseas territory]] of the United States, in men's international [[association football|football]] and is controlled by the [[Guam Football Association]]. Affiliated with [[FIFA]] since 1996 and a member of [[Asian Football Confederation|AFC]] since 1991.

Guam reached its highest ever position in the FIFA World Ranking at 146th in 2015 after victories over [[Turkmenistan national football team|Turkmenistan]] and [[India men's national football team|India]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.fifa.com/associations/association=gum/ranking/gender=m|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131031202743/http://www.fifa.com/associations/association=gum/ranking/gender=m/|url-status=dead|archive-date=31 October 2013|title=FIFA/Coca-Cola World Ranking − Guam Men's Ranking|website=fifa.com|publisher=FIFA|date=6 August 2015|access-date=31 August 2015}}</ref> Guam's first [[FIFA World Cup qualification]] match was played against [[Iran national football team]] at [[Tabriz]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Biggest margin victories/losses |url=http://www.fifa.com/mm/document/fifafacts/stats-matches/52/00/64/170429-factsheet-fifacompetitionsbiggestmarginvictories.pdf |access-date=2023-11-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130521083035/http://www.fifa.com/mm/document/fifafacts/stats-matches/52/00/64/170429-factsheet-fifacompetitionsbiggestmarginvictories.pdf |archive-date=May 21, 2013 }}</ref>

==History==

=== Beginnings (1960s–1990s) ===

Football in Guam began in the 1960s, on the initiative of two pioneers: an Irish priest, Tony Gillespie, and an owner of a construction company, Charles Whang, who settled in Guam and created the Guam Soccer Cup before becoming the first president of the [[Guam Football Association]] in 1975.

Guam played its first international match at the [[Football at the 1975 South Pacific Games|1975 Pacific Games]] against [[Solomon Islands national football team|Solomon Islands]], losing 5–1. This result was followed by an 11–0 loss to [[Fiji national football team|Fiji]], eliminating them from the competition. In the [[Football at the 1979 South Pacific Games|1979 Games]], Guam again lost twice to [[New Caledonia national football team|New Caledonia]] 11–1 and [[New Hebrides national football team|New Hebrides]] 5–0. Progressing to the Consolation Tournament, Guam registered their first victories, beating [[Western Samoa national football team|Western Samoa]] 4–2, and [[Tuvalu national football team|Tuvalu]] 7–2 before losing again to [[New Hebrides national football team|New Hebrides]], 5–1.

=== AFC and FIFA Member (1991–2011) ===
Despite being considered part of Oceania, and having never faced an Asian team to that point, Guam became an associate member of the [[Asian Football Confederation|AFC]] in 1991 but continued to participate in the [[Pacific Games]].<ref>{{cite web |title=HISTORY OF GFA |url=https://guamfa.com/contact-us/history-of-gfa/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220415204416/https://guamfa.com/contact-us/history-of-gfa/ |archive-date=15 April 2022 |access-date=4 February 2022 |website=Guam FA}}</ref> In 1996, Guam became both a full member of the AFC and of [[FIFA]].

As a full member of the AFC and FIFA, Guam played its first matches against Asian teams as part of the [[1996 AFC Asian Cup qualification]] losing all three matches against [[South Korea national football team|South Korea]] (9–0), [[Vietnam national football team|Vietnam]] (9–0), and [[Chinese Taipei national football team|Chinese Taipei]] (9–2).

In 1998, Guam played in the [[Micronesian Games]], though the tournament was a 9-a-side competition with 80 minute matches. Guam finished top of the group stage but lost 3–0 to [[Northern Mariana Islands national football team|Northern Mariana Islands]] in the final.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Cruickshank |first1=Mark |title=Micronesian Games 1998 |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesm/micronesia98.html |website=RSSSF |access-date=2 February 2023 |archive-date=13 July 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220713075238/https://www.rsssf.org/tablesm/micronesia98.html |url-status=live }}</ref> Guam entered the [[2000 AFC Asian Cup qualification|2000 Asian Cup qualification]] but finished last in their group again, losing to Vietnam (11–0), [[China national football team|China]] (19–0), and [[Philippines national football team|Philippines]] (2–0).

Guam became one of the founding members of the [[East Asian Football Federation]] in 2002 and participated in the qualifiers for the inaugural [[2003 East Asian Football Championship]], finishing last again without registering a goal. The same is true for the [[2004 AFC Asian Cup qualification]], beaten by [[Bhutan national football team|Bhutan]] and [[Mongolia national football team|Mongolia]]. For the [[2006 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)|2006 World Cup qualifiers]], Guam entered and was scheduled to face [[Nepal national football team|Nepal]] in the first round but both Nepal and Guam withdrew for financial reasons before their first match.

In the [[2005 East Asian Football Championship]] qualifiers, Guam lost all four matches heavily, against [[Chinese Taipei national football team|Chinese Taipei]] (9–0), [[Hong Kong national football team|Hong Kong]] (15–0), [[Mongolia national football team|Mongolia]] (4–1), and [[North Korea national football team|North Korea]] (21–0). Following these defeats, Guam entered the [[2006 AFC Challenge Cup]], a competition designed for ''emerging nations'' to help develop their football. Guam again lost all three matches to [[Palestine national football team|Palestine]] (11–0), [[Bangladesh national football team|Bangladesh]] (3–0), and [[Cambodia national football team|Cambodia]] (3–0).

Guam advanced to the second stage of qualifying in the [[2008 East Asian Football Championship]] by beating [[Northern Mariana Islands national football team|Northern Marianas]] 12–2 over two legs but they were eliminated in the next round by [[Chinese Taipei national football team|Chinese Taipei]] (10–0), and [[Hong Kong national football team|Hong Kong]] (15–1). In the [[2010 East Asian Football Championship|2010 East Asian Football Championship qualifiers]], Guam managed to get its first victory over a FIFA member, beating [[Mongolia national football team|Mongolia]] 1–0. They would go on to top the group before being eliminated at the next stage. Guam returned to the [[Football at the 2011 Pacific Games – Men's tournament|2011 Pacific Games]] after a sixteen-year absence, but only managed to beat [[American Samoa national football team|American Samoa]] 2–0, finishing second last and failing to advance to the knock-out stages.

=== Building the football structure (2012–present) ===

[[Gary White (footballer)|Gary White]] became Guam's manager in 2012 and called-up players of Guamanian origin based in the United States like [[Ryan Guy]] and [[A. J. DeLaGarza]] to improve the level of his team. In the [[2013 EAFF East Asian Cup]], ''Matao'' managed to advance past the first round by beating [[Northern Mariana Islands national football team|Northern Marianas]] (3–1) and [[Macao national football team|Macao]] (3–0). Guam then participated in the [[2012 Philippine Peace Cup]], replacing [[Hong Kong national football team|Hong Kong]], losing against [[Philippines national football team|Philippines]] (1–0) and [[Chinese Taipei national football team|Chinese Taipei]] (2–0) but beat [[Macao national football team|Macao]] (3–0) in its final group game to finish third. Guam advanced to the second qualifying stage of both the [[2015 EAFF East Asian Cup|2015]] and [[2017 EAFF E-1 Football Championship|2017 EAFF Championship]] finishing third and fourth respectively and failing to advance the final competition.
{{OSM Location map 
| coord        = {{coord|13.450305725187372|144.77103148042326}}
| zoom         = 10
| width        = 370
| height       = 370
| mark-size1   = 16
| shape1       = n-circle
| caption      = Map of Guam stadium {{col-begin}}{{Col-3}}{{Ubl
  | {{#invoke:overlay|icon|1|#B51210}} [[Dededo]]
  | {{#invoke:overlay|icon|2|#B51210}} [[Hagåtña, Guam|Hagåtña]] 
  | {{#invoke:overlay|icon|3|#B51210}} [[Tumon, Guam|Tumon]]
  | {{#invoke:overlay|icon|4|#B51210}} [[Yona, Guam|Yona]]
  }}{{col-end}}

| mark-coord1        = {{Coord|13.512319599496756|144.83182717095784}}
| shape-color1       = #B51210
| mark-title1        = [[GFA National Training Center|Guam Football Association National Training Center]]
| mark-description1  = [[Dededo]]
| mark-coord2        = {{Coord|13.500681657691478|144.77918992366216}}
| shape-color2       = #B51210
| mark-title2        = [[Guam National Football Stadium]]
| mark-description2  = [[Hagåtña, Guam|Hagåtña]]
| mark-coord3        = {{Coord|13.50137404158798|144.795157365702}}
| shape-color3       = #B51210
| mark-title3        = Washington High playing fields
| mark-description3  = [[Tumon, Guam|Tumon]]
| mark-coord4        = {{Coord|13.418813640967446|144.73708352676948}}
| shape-color4       = #B51210
| mark-title4        = [[Leo Palace Resort Main Field|Leo Palace Resort; Main Field]]
| mark-description4  = [[Yona, Guam|Yona]]
}}

In [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)|2018 FIFA World Cup qualification]], Guam beat [[Turkmenistan national football team|Turkmenistan]] (1–0) and [[India men's national football team|India]] (2–1), finishing fourth in their group and qualifying for the [[2019 AFC Asian Cup qualification – third round|2019 Asian Cup third round qualifiers]], although Guam withdrew before the draw took place. Guam then failed to progress past the first qualifying round for the [[2019 EAFF E-1 Football Championship|2019 EAFF Championship]].

During the [[2022 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)|2022 FIFA World Cup qualifiers]], Guam beat [[Bhutan national football team|Bhutan]] 5–1 over two legs but lost all eight of their second round matches and finished bottom of their group, scoring two goals and conceding 32.

After nearly not playing an international match for a year, Guam participated in the first round of the [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC first round|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]] against [[Singapore national football team|Singapore]], where they would ultimately lost 3–1 on aggregate.

During the [[2025 EAFF E-1 Football Championship#Preliminary competition|2025 EAFF E-1 Football Championship preliminary]], Guam won their first match against [[Macau national football team|Macau]] on 14 December 2024 in a 2–1 win, its the first time they won against different opponents aside from [[Northern Mariana Islands national football team|Northern Mariana Islands]].

==== 2026 FIFA Series ====
The [[2026 FIFA Series (men's matches)|men's competition]] of the 2026 FIFA Series was split into nine different sections hosted across eight countries. In November 2025, FIFA announced that [[Puerto Rico national football team|Puerto Rico]] would host one such section in [[Bayamón, Puerto Rico|Bayamón]], in which Guam, the [[United States Virgin Islands national soccer team|U.S. Virgin Islands]] and [[American Samoa national football team|American Samoa]] would also compete. The [[2026 FIFA Series (men's matches)#Puerto Rico|Puerto Rican section of the tournament]] was held in a four-match format (semi-finals, third-place match and final) in late March 2026. Guam finished third in the section after losing to hosts Puerto Rico in its semi-final but defeating American Samoa in the third-place match.<ref name="2026 FIFA Series overview">{{cite web |author=<!-- not stated --> |date=March 3, 2026 |title=FIFA Series 2026: Everything you need to know |url=https://www.fifa.com/en/news/articles/fifa-series-everything-you-need-to-know |website=fifa.com |location= |publisher=[[FIFA]] |access-date=April 1, 2026}}</ref><ref name="2026 FIFA Series champions">{{cite web |author=<!-- not stated --> |date=March 29, 2026 |title=De Leon propels Puerto Rico to title |url=https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/series/articles/puerto-rico-champions-guam-finish-third |website=fifa.com |location= |publisher=[[FIFA]] |access-date=April 1, 2026}}</ref>

==Team image==

=== Nicknames ===
Until 2011, the nickname of the Guamanian selection was ''Chamorros'', in reference to the local population of Guam. When [[Gary White (footballer)|Gary White]] took charge of Guam, the team has changed their nickname to ''Matao'', referring to highest social class in ancient Chamorro society in the [[Chamorro language]]. White also promoted the use of the Guam pledge before each match of the selection, referred to as the ''Inifresi''.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Vénétitay |first1=Guillaume |title=Interview with Gary White |url=https://www.sofoot.com/on-a-cree-cette-culture-du-football-a-guam-219494.html |website=So Foot |date=March 24, 2016 |access-date=4 February 2022 |archive-date=4 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220204113137/https://www.sofoot.com/on-a-cree-cette-culture-du-football-a-guam-219494.html |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |last1=Bate |first1=Adam |title=Gary White: Life in Guam, coaching in Japan and dreams of England |url=https://www.skysports.com/football/news/11095/10215535/gary-white-life-in-guam-coaching-in-japan-and-dreams-of-england |website=Sky Sports |access-date=4 February 2022 |archive-date=4 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220204113137/https://www.skysports.com/football/news/11095/10215535/gary-white-life-in-guam-coaching-in-japan-and-dreams-of-england |url-status=live }}</ref>

==Results and fixtures==
{{Main|Guam national football team results}}

The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

===2026===
{{footballbox collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA Series (men's matches)#Puerto Rico|2026 FIFA Series]]
|date       = 25 March 
|time       = {{UTZ|20:00|-4}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|PUR}} 
|score      = 4–0
|team2      = {{fb|GUM}}
|goals1     =
*[[Wilfredo Rivera (footballer)|W. Rivera]] {{goal|52||61}}
*[[Ricardo Rivera|R. Rivera]] {{goal|57}}
*[[Leandro Antonetti|Antonetti]] {{goal|76|pen.}}
|goals2     =
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/10017/292871/292872/400021854?date=2026-03-25
|stadium    = [[Juan Ramón Loubriel Stadium]]
|location   = [[Bayamón, Puerto Rico|Bayamón]], Puerto Rico
|attendance = 
|referee    = José Corporán ([[Dominican Football Federation|Dominican Republic]])
|result     = L
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA Series (men's matches)#Puerto Rico|2026 FIFA Series]]
|date       = 28 March 
|time       = {{UTZ|16:00|-4}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|ASA}}
|score      = 0–6
|team2      = {{fb|GUM}} 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =*[[Ali'i Mitchell|Mitchell]] {{Goal|3|o.g.}}
*[[Levi Berg|Berg]] {{Goal|13||35}}
*[[Elijah Ochoa|Ochoa]] {{Goal|39|pen.}}
*[[Alec Taitague|Taitague]] {{Goal|57}}
*[[James Gomez (footballer, born 2004)|Gomez]] {{Goal|86}}
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/10017/292871/292872/400021857?date=2026-03-28
|stadium    = [[Juan Ramón Loubriel Stadium]]
|location   = [[Bayamón, Puerto Rico|Bayamón]], Puerto Rico
|attendance = 
|referee    = [[José Torres (referee)|José Torres]] ([[Puerto Rican Football Federation|Puerto Rico]])
|result     = W
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
| format     = 1
| round      = [[Exhibition Match|Friendly]]
| date       = 3 June  
| time       = {{UTZ|19:30|8}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|PHI}}
| score      = 5–1
| report     = 
| team2      = {{fb|GUM}}
| goals1     =
*[[Cole Mrowka|Mrowka]] {{goal|5}}
*[[Jarvey Gayoso|Gayoso]] {{goal|18}}
*[[Santiago Rublico|Rublico]] {{goal|44}}
*[[Andre Leipold|Leipold]] {{goal|64||90+2}}
| goals2     = 
*[[Levi Berg|Berg]] {{goal|60}}
| stadium    = [[Rizal Memorial Stadium]]
| location   = [[Manila]], Philippines
| attendance = 2,243
| referee    = [[Songkran Bunmeekiart|Songkran Bunmeekiart]] ([[Football Association of Thailand|Thailand]])
| result     = L
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
| format     = 1
| round      = [[Exhibition Match|Friendly]]
| date       = 6 June 
| time       = {{UTZ|19:30|8}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|GUM}}
| score      = 1–6
| report     = 
| team2      = {{fb|MYA}}
| goals1     =
*[[James Gomez (footballer, born 2004)|J. Gomez]] {{goal|90+1|pen.}}
| goals2     = 
*[[Soe Moe Kyaw]] {{goal|13}}
*[[Maung Maung Lwin]] {{goal|23}}
*[[Win Naing Tun]] {{goal|35||53}}
*[[Zaw Win Thein]] {{goal|57}}
*[[Shai Wanna Aung]] {{goal|86}}
| stadium    = [[Rizal Memorial Stadium]]
| location   = [[Manila]], Philippines
| attendance = 
| referee    = Nawal Zubir ([[Football Association of Malaysia|Malaysia]])
| result     = L
}}

==Coaching staff==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! style="color:#FFFFFF;background:#00297B" |Position
! style="color:#FFFFFF;background:#00297B" |Name
|-
| Head coach
| {{flagicon|Guam}}{{flagicon|Philippines}} [[Ross Awa]]
|-
| Assistant coach
| {{flagicon|USA}} Christopher Malenab
|-
| Assistant coach
| {{flagicon|Austria}} Manfred Preschern
|-
| Goalkeeper coach
| {{flagicon|Netherlands}} Jeroen Bos
|-
| Video Analyst
| {{flagicon|USA}} Christopher Malenab
|-
| Team Doctor
| {{flagicon|Guam}} Dr. Luis Cruz
|-
| Physiotherapist
| {{flagicon|USA}} Dr. Jon Thompson
|-
| Physiotherapist
| {{flagicon|PHI}} Walfred Javier
|-
| Team Manager
| {{flagicon|Guam}} Vance Manibusan
|-
| High Performance Manager
| {{flagicon|Russia}} Pavel Gubenko
|-
| Media Officer
| {{flagicon|Guam}} Jill Espiritu
|-
| Equipment Director
| {{flagicon|Guam}} Thomas Castro
|}

===Coaching history===
{{div col|colwidth=25em}}
*{{flagicon|NIR}} [[Willie McFaul]] (1999–2003)
*{{flagicon|JPN}} [[Sugao Kambe]] (2003–2005)
*{{flagicon|JPN}} [[Norio Tsukitate]] (2005–2009)
*{{flagicon|JPN}} [[Kazuo Uchida]] (2011–2012)
*{{flagicon|ENG}} [[Gary White (footballer)|Gary White]] (2012–2016)
*{{flagicon|USA}} [[Darren Sawatzky]] (2016–2017)
*{{flagicon|AUS}} [[Karl Dodd]] (2017–2021)
*{{flagicon|KOR}} [[Seo Dong-won (footballer, born 1973)|Seo Dong-won]] (2021)
*{{flagicon|KOR}} [[Kim Sang-hoon]] (2021–2023)
*{{flagicon|GUM}} [[Ross Awa]] (2023–present)
{{div col end}}

==Players==
===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[2025 EAFF E-1 Football Championship|2025 EAFF E-1 Football Championship preliminary competition]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Head Coach Kim calls up 22 to Matao for AFC Asian Cup Playoff |url=https://guamfa.com/head-coach-kim-calls-up-22-to-matao-for-afc-playoff/ |website=GuamFA.com |publisher=Guam FA |access-date=9 October 2021 |archive-date=9 October 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211009111525/https://guamfa.com/head-coach-kim-calls-up-22-to-matao-for-afc-playoff/ |url-status=live }}</ref>

''Caps and goals correct as of December 14, 2024, after the match against [[Macau national football team|Macau]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=1|pos=GK|name=[[John-Michael Guidroz]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|2004|1|1}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[University of West Florida]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=18|pos=GK|name=[[Josiah Jones (footballer)|Josiah Jones]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|2006|11|28}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Bank of Guam Strykers FC]]|clubnat=GUM}}
{{nat fs g player|no=21|pos=GK|name=[[Dallas Jaye]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|1993|6|19}}|caps=22|goals=0|club=''[[free agent]]''|clubnat=}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Zaine Rocca]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|2007|6|28}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=Total Futbol Academy|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=2|pos=DF|name=[[Morgan McKenna]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|2003|1|16}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Muskingum University]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=3|pos=DF|name=[[Takumi Ito (footballer)|Takumi Ito]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|2000|2|3}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Ezra F.C.|Ezra]]|clubnat=LAO}}
{{nat fs g player|no=4|pos=DF|name=[[Kyle Halehale]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|2002|6|3}}|caps=13|goals=0|club=[[Mercyhurst University]]|clubnat=USA}} 
{{nat fs g player|no=5|pos=DF|name=[[Leon Morimoto]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|2001|12|18}}|caps=5|goals=1|club=[[Hunters FC Mongolia|Hunters Mongolia]]|clubnat=MGL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=13|pos=DF|name=[[Anthony Quidachay]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|2002|6|11}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Chatham Cougars]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=15|pos=DF|name=[[Shane Healy (footballer)|Shane Healy]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|1998|7|3}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Bank of Guam Strykers FC]]|clubnat=GUM}}
{{nat fs g player|no=20|pos=DF|name=[[Levi Buckwalter]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|2004|12|29}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Wings FC]]|clubnat=GUM}}
{{nat fs g player|no=22|pos=DF|name=[[Isiah Lagutang]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|1997|8|3}}|caps=13|goals=1|club=[[Bank of Guam Strykers FC]]|clubnat=GUM}}
{{nat fs g player|no=23|pos=DF|name=[[Jonahan Romero]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|1988|3|17}}|caps=34|goals=0|club=SYC United|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Nate Lee (footballer)|Nate Lee]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|1994|5|6}}|caps=11|goals=0|club=[[FC Frederick]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Dane Agustin]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|2006|1|23}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[San Antonio FC Academy]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=6|pos=MF|name=[[Joey Ciochetto]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|1996|10|22}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Irvine Zeta FC|Irvine Zeta]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=8|pos=MF|name=[[Alec Taitague]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|2000|5|9}}|caps=31|goals=3|club=[[Roanoke Maroons]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=10|pos=MF|name=[[Jason Cunliffe]] ([[Captain (association football)|captain]])|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|1983|10|23}}|caps=69|goals=26|club=[[Bank of Guam Strykers FC]]|clubnat=GUM}}
{{nat fs g player|no=11|pos=MF|name=[[Nainoa Norton]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|2004|4|3}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Olivet Nazarene University]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=12|pos=MF|name=[[Nathan Sablan]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|1993|8|10}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Mendiola F.C. 1991|Mendiola 1991]]|clubnat=PHI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=14|pos=MF|name=[[Jason Castro (footballer)|Jason Castro]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|2007|7|6}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Strikers FC Irvine]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[John Matkin]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|1986|4|20}}|caps=30|goals=2|club=''[[Free agent]]''|clubnat=}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Jude Bischoff]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|1992|4|26}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Guam Shipyard]]|clubnat=GUM}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Travis Nicklaw]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|1993|12|21}}|caps=36|goals=1|club=''[[Free agent]]''|clubnat=}}
{{nat fs g break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=7|pos=FW|name=[[James Gomez (footballer, born 2004)|James Gomez]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|2004|9|15}}|caps=5|goals=3|club=[[Claremont McKenna College]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=9|pos=FW|name=[[Oz Rocca]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|2004|8|10}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Cal State Bakersfield Roadrunners]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=16|pos=FW|name=[[Levi Berg]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|2008|4|15}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Guam Shipyard]]|clubnat=GUM}}
{{nat fs g player|no=17|pos=FW|name=[[Shuntaro Suzuki]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|2005|10|21}}|caps=3|goals=1|club=[[Wings FC]]|clubnat=GUM}}
{{nat fs g player|no=19|pos=FW|name=[[Daniel Glasscock]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|2004|5|19}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Manhoben Lalahi FC]]|clubnat=GUM}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Marcus Lopez]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|1992|2|9}}|caps=39|goals=7|club=[[Bank of Guam Strykers FC]]|clubnat=GUM}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Eddie Na]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|1996|2|12}}|caps=8|goals=0|club=[[Tacoma Stars]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Ka'eo Gonsalves]]|age={{Birth date and age|mf=yes|2005|1|6}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Richmond Kickers]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have also been called up to the Guam squad within last 12 months.

{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs break}}
<sup>INJ</sup> Withdrew due to injury<br>
<sup>PRE</sup> Preliminary squad<br>
<sup>RET</sup> Retired from the national team<br>
<sup>SUS</sup> Serving suspension<br>
{{nat fs end}}

==Player records==
{{Updated|June 6, 2026}}
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Guam.''

===Most appearances===
[[File:Jason Cunliffe.JPG|thumb|200px|right|[[Jason Cunliffe]] is Guam's top goalscorer and their most-capped player]]

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:150px;"|Player
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Goals
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
|1
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Jason Cunliffe]]'''
|74
|26
|2006–present
|-
|2
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Marcus Lopez]]'''
|43
|7
|2012–present
|-
|3
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Ian Mariano]]
|42
|3
|2007–2019
|-
|4
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Dylan Naputi]]
|40
|4
|2011–2019
|-
|5
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Travis Nicklaw]]'''
|39
|1
|2012–present
|-
|rowspan=3|6
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Dominic Gadia]]
|36
|0
|2003–2019
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Mark Chargualaf]]
|36
|0
|2007–2021
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Jonahan Romero]]'''
|36
|0
|2012–present
|-
|9
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Shawn Nicklaw]]
|35
|2
|2012–2019
|-
|rowspan=2|10
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Ryan Guy]]
|33
|4
|2012–2016
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Micah Paulino]]
|33
|0
|2008–2016
|}

===Top goalscorers===

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:150px;"|Player
!width=50px|Goals
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Ratio
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
|1
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Jason Cunliffe]]'''
|26
|74
|{{#expr:26/74 round 2}}
|2006–present
|- 
|2
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Matthew Naputi]]
|13
|—
|—
|1998–2005
|-
|3
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Zachary Pangelinan]]
|8
|12
|{{#expr:8/12 round 2}}
|2005–2008
|-
|4
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Marcus Lopez]]'''
|7
|43
|{{#expr:7/43 round 2}}
|2012–present
|-
|5
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Joshua Borja]]
|5
|15
|{{#expr:5/15 round 2}}
|2009–2013
|-
|rowspan=5|6
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[James Gomez (footballer, born 2004)|James Gomez]]'''
|4
|9
|{{#expr:4/9 round 2}}
|2021–present
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Christopher Mendiola]]
|4
|11
|{{#expr:4/11 round 2}}
|2007–2009
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Shane Malcolm]]
|4
|29
|{{#expr:4/29 round 2}}
|2014–2019
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Ryan Guy]]
|4
|33
|{{#expr:4/33 round 2}}
|2012–2016
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Dylan Naputi]]
|4
|40
|{{#expr:4/40 round 2}}
|2011–2019
|}

==Competitive record==
{{Main|Guam national football team results}}

=== FIFA World Cup ===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|+
|-
! colspan="9" |[[FIFA World Cup]]
! rowspan="39" |
! colspan="6" |[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]]
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|F|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|A|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|F|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|A|Goals against}}
|-
| {{flagicon|Uruguay}} [[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]]
| rowspan="16" colspan="8" | ''Not a FIFA member''
| rowspan="16" colspan="6" | ''Not a FIFA member''
|-
| {{flagicon|Italy|1861}} [[1934 FIFA World Cup|1934]]
|-
| {{flagicon|France|1794}} [[1938 FIFA World Cup|1938]]
|-
| {{flagicon|Brazil|1889}} [[1950 FIFA World Cup|1950]]
|-
| {{flagicon|Switzerland}} [[1954 FIFA World Cup|1954]]
|-
| {{flagicon|Sweden}} [[1958 FIFA World Cup|1958]]
|-
| {{flagicon|Chile}} [[1962 FIFA World Cup|1962]]
|-
|{{flagicon|England}} [[1966 FIFA World Cup|1966]]
|-
| {{flagicon|Mexico}} [[1970 FIFA World Cup|1970]]
|-
| {{flagicon|West Germany}} [[1974 FIFA World Cup|1974]]
|-
| {{flagicon|Argentina}} [[1978 FIFA World Cup|1978]]
|-
| {{flagicon|Spain}} [[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982]]
|-
| {{flagicon|Mexico}} [[1986 FIFA World Cup|1986]]
|-
| {{flagicon|Italy|1946}} [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]
|-
| {{flagicon|United States of America}} [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
|-
| {{flagicon|South Korea|1997}}{{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
| colspan="8" | ''Did not qualify''
| 2
| 0
| 0
| 2
| 0 
| 35
|-
| {{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
| colspan="8" rowspan="3" | ''Withdrew'' 
| colspan="8" rowspan="3" | ''Withdrew'' 
|-
| {{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
|-
| {{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
|-
| {{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
| colspan="8" rowspan="3" | ''Did not qualify''
| 8
| 2
| 1
| 5
| 3
| 16
|-
| {{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
| 10
| 1
| 0
| 9
| 7
| 33
|-
| {{flagicon|Canada}}{{flagicon|Mexico}}{{flagicon|United States of America}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
| 2
| 0
| 0
| 2
| 1
| 3
|-
| {{flagicon|Morocco}}{{flagicon|Portugal}}{{flagicon|Spain}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
| colspan="9" rowspan="2" | ''To be determined''
| colspan="9" rowspan="2" | ''To be determined''
|-
| {{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total
!
!{{Tooltip|0/4|Number of tournaments qualified for}}
!
!
!
!
!
!
!22
!3
!1
!18
!11
!87
|}

=== AFC Asian Cup ===
{|class="wikitable nowrap" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[AFC Asian Cup]]
! style="width:1%;" rowspan="22"|
!colspan=6|[[AFC Asian Cup qualification|Qualification]] record 
|-
!Year
!Result 
!Position 
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D*|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GS|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D*|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Hong Kong|1955}} [[1956 AFC Asian Cup|1956]] ||colspan="8" rowspan=9| ''Not a member of the AFC'' ||colspan="6" rowspan=9| ''Not a member of the AFC''
|-
|{{flagicon|South Korea|1949}} [[1960 AFC Asian Cup|1960]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Israel}} [[1964 AFC Asian Cup|1964]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Iran|1964}} [[1968 AFC Asian Cup|1968]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Thailand}} [[1972 AFC Asian Cup|1972]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Iran|1964}} [[1976 AFC Asian Cup|1976]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Kuwait}} [[1980 AFC Asian Cup|1980]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Singapore}} [[1984 AFC Asian Cup|1984]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[1988 AFC Asian Cup|1988]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Japan|1870}} [[1992 AFC Asian Cup|1992]] ||colspan="8"| ''Did not enter'' ||colspan="6"| ''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|United Arab Emirates}} [[1996 AFC Asian Cup|1996]]||colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not qualify''||3|||0||0||3||2||27
|-
|{{flagicon|Lebanon}} [[2000 AFC Asian Cup|2000]]||3|||0||0||3||0||32
|-
|{{flagicon|China}} [[2004 AFC Asian Cup|2004]]||2|||0||0||2||0||11
|-
|{{flagicon|Indonesia}} {{flagicon|Malaysia}} {{flagicon|Thailand}} {{flagicon|Vietnam}} [[2007 AFC Asian Cup|2007]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''||colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2011 AFC Asian Cup|2011]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''||colspan=8 rowspan=2|[[AFC Challenge Cup]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Australia}} [[2015 AFC Asian Cup|2015]]
|-
|{{flagicon|United Arab Emirates}} [[2019 AFC Asian Cup|2019]]||colspan=8 |''Withdrew during qualification''||8||2||1||5||3||16
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2023 AFC Asian Cup|2023]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify'' ||12||1||0||11||8||36
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2027 AFC Asian Cup|2027]]||colspan=8|''Withdrew during qualification''
|2||0||0||2||1||3
|-
!'''Total'''||'''0/18'''||colspan=7|'''—'''||'''30'''||'''3'''||'''1'''||'''25'''||'''14'''||'''125'''
|}
* [[2019 AFC Asian Cup|2019]]&nbsp;– Withdrew from the third tournament due to financial constraints.

===East Asian Football Championship===

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[East Asian Football Championship]] record
! rowspan=2|
!colspan=6|Preliminary competition
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
! colspan="16"|East Asian Football Championship
|-
|{{flagicon|Japan}} [[2003 East Asian Football Championship|2003]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=4| ''Did not qualify''
!rowspan=4|
|4||0||0||4||0||22
|-
|{{flagicon|South Korea}} [[2005 East Asian Football Championship|2005]]
|4||0||0||4||1||49
|-
|{{flagicon|China}} [[2008 East Asian Football Championship|2008]]
|5||2||0||3||15||32
|-
|{{flagicon|Japan}} [[2010 East Asian Football Championship|2010]]
|6||2||1||3||9||28
|-
! colspan="16"|EAFF East Asian Cup
|-
|{{flagicon|South Korea}} [[2013 EAFF East Asian Cup|2013]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2| ''Did not qualify''
!rowspan=2|
|6||2||1||3||8||18
|-
|{{flagicon|China}} [[2015 EAFF East Asian Cup|2015]]
|6||3||2||1||10||6
|-
! colspan="16"|EAFF E-1 Football Championship
|-
|{{flagicon|Japan}} [[2017 EAFF E-1 Football Championship|2017]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2| ''Did not qualify''
!rowspan=10|
|3||0||0||3||2||7
|-
|{{flagicon|South Korea}} [[2019 EAFF E-1 Football Championship|2019]]
|3||1||1||1||5||3
|-
|{{flagicon|JPN}} [[2022 EAFF E-1 Football Championship|2022]]
|colspan=8|''Did not participate''
|colspan=6|''Not held''
|-
|{{flagicon|KOR}} [[2025 EAFF E-1 Football Championship|2025]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
||2||1||0||1||2||6
|-
!'''Total'''
! –
!'''0/9'''
!'''0'''
!'''0'''
!'''0''' 
!'''0''' 
!'''0'''
!'''0'''
!'''39'''
!'''11'''
!'''5'''
!'''23'''
!'''52'''
!'''171'''
|}

===AFC Challenge Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[AFC Challenge Cup]] record
!rowspan=39|
!colspan=6|Qualification record
|-
!Year
!Result
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Bangladesh}} [[2006 AFC Challenge Cup|2006]]||Group stage||16th||3||0||0||3||0||17
|colspan=6|''No qualification''
|-
|{{flagicon|India}} [[2008 AFC Challenge Cup|2008]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|3
|0
|0
|3
|4
|18
|-
|{{flagicon|Sri Lanka}} [[2010 AFC Challenge Cup|2010]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6 rowspan=2|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Nepal}} [[2012 AFC Challenge Cup|2012]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Maldives}} [[2014 AFC Challenge Cup|2014]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|3
|1
|0
|2
|3
|9
|-
!Total||Group stage||1/5||3||0||0||3||0||17||6||1||0||5||7|||27
|-
|}

===Pacific Games===

{{col-begin}}
{{col-break}}

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[Football at the Pacific Games|Pacific Games]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Fiji}} [[Football at the 1963 South Pacific Games|1963]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=4|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|New Caledonia|1853}} [[Football at the 1966 South Pacific Games|1966]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Papua New Guinea}} [[Football at the 1969 South Pacific Games|1969]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Tahiti}} [[Football at the 1971 South Pacific Games|1971]]
|-
|style="border: 3px solid red"|{{flagicon|Guam}} [[Football at the 1975 South Pacific Games|1975]]
| rowspan=2|Group stage
| 7th
| 2
| 0
| 0
| 2
| 1
| 16
|-
|{{flagicon|Fiji}} [[Football at the 1979 South Pacific Games|1979]]
| 6th
| 5
| 2
| 0
| 3
| 13
| 25
|-
|{{flagicon|Western Samoa}} [[Football at the 1983 South Pacific Games|1983]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|New Caledonia|1853}} [[Football at the 1987 South Pacific Games|1987]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Papua New Guinea}} [[Football at the 1991 South Pacific Games|1991]]
| rowspan=2|Group stage
| 8th
| 4
| 0
| 0
| 3
| 1
| 52
|-
|{{flagicon|Tahiti}} [[Football at the 1995 South Pacific Games|1995]]
| 8th
| 3
| 0
| 0
| 3
| 0
| 23
|-
|{{flagicon|Fiji}} [[Football at the 2003 South Pacific Games|2003]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Samoa}} [[Football at the 2007 South Pacific Games|2007]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Fiji}} [[Men's Football at the 2011 Pacific Games|2011]]
| Group stage
| 9th
| 5
| 1
| 1
| 3
| 4
| 21
|-
|{{flagicon|PNG}} [[Men's Football at the 2015 Pacific Games|2015]]
|colspan=8|''N/A – tournament was U23''
|-
|{{flagicon|Samoa}} [[Men's Football at the 2019 Pacific Games|2019]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
!'''Total'''
!Group stage
!'''5/15'''
!'''19'''
!'''3'''
!'''1''' 
!'''14''' 
!'''19'''
!'''147'''
|}
{{col-break}}

===Pacific Mini Games===

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[Football at the South Pacific Games|Pacific Mini Games]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Solomon Islands}} [[Football at the 1981 South Pacific Mini Games|1981]]
|colspan=8|''did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Vanuatu}} [[Football at the 1993 South Pacific Mini Games|1993]]
|Group stage
|8th
|3
|0
|0
|3
|1
|35
|-
|{{flagicon|Vanuatu}} [[Football at the 2017 Pacific Mini Games – Men's tournament|2017]]
|colspan=8|''did not enter''
|-
!Total
!Group Stage
!1/3
!3
!0
!0
!3
!1
!35
|}
{{col-end}}

===Micronesian Games===

{|class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[Football at the Micronesian Games|Micronesian Games]] record
|-
!Year
!Result
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D*|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GS|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|- style="background:Silver;"
|{{flagicon|Palau}} [[Football at the Micronesian Games#1998|1998]] ||'''Runners-up'''||'''2nd'''||'''6'''||'''5'''||'''0'''||'''1'''||'''52'''||'''7'''
|-
|{{flagicon|Pohnpei}} [[Football at the Micronesian Games#2014|2014]]|| colspan="8" rowspan="2"| ''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Yap}} [[Football at the Micronesian Games#2018|2018]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Marshall Islands}} [[2024 Micronesian Games|2024]]|| colspan="8"| ''Football was not part of the Games''
|-
!Total||Runners-up||1/3||6||5||0||1||43||5
|}

== Head-to-head record ==
''As of 6 June 2026''
{| class="wikitable sortable"
|+
!Opponent
!First
!Last
!P
!W
!D
!L
!GF
!GA
!GD
!Confederation
|- bgcolor=#dfd
|{{fb|ASA|2013}}
|2011
|2026
|2
|2
|0
|0
|8
|0
|0
|[[Oceania Football Confederation|OFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|ARU|2013}}
|2014
|2014
|2
|0
|1
|1
|2
|4
|–2
|[[CONCACAF]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|AUS}}
|2012
|2012
|1
|0
|0
|1
|0
|9
|–9
|[[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|BAN}}
|2006
|2006
|1
|0
|0
|1
|0
|3
|–3
|[[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|BHU}}
|2003
|2019
|3
|1
|0
|2
|5
|7
|–2
|[[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|CAM}}
|2006
|2021
|4
|1
|0
|3
|3
|6
|–3
|[[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|CHN}}
|2000
|2021
|3
|0
|0
|3
|0
|33
|–33
|[[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|TPE}}
|1996
|2016
|12
|2
|1
|5
|13
|52
|–39
|[[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|FIJ}}
|1975
|1996
|2
|0
|0
|2
|1
|19
|–18
|[[Oceania Football Confederation|OFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|HKG}}
|2003
|2024
|9
|0
|1
|8
|4
|64
|–60
|[[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|IND}}
|2013
|2015
|3
|1
|0
|2
|2
|6
|–4
|[[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|IRN}}
|2000
|2015
|3
|0
|0
|3
|0
|31
|–31
|[[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|PRK}}
|2005
|2016
|5
|0
|0
|5
|3
|42
|–39
|[[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|KOR}}
|1996
|1996
|1
|0
|0
|1
|0
|9
|–9
|[[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]]
|- bgcolor=#ffd
|{{fb|LAO}}
|2013
|2013
|1
|0
|1
|0
|1
|1
|0
|[[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]]
|- bgcolor=#dfd
|{{fb|MAC}}
|2003
|2024
|7
|3
|2
|2
|10
|7
|3
|[[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|MDV}}
|2019
|2019
|2
|0
|0
|2
|1
|4
|–3
|[[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|MGL}}
|2003
|2018
|7
|2
|1
|4
|7
|17
|–17
|[[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|MYA}}
|2013
|2026
|2
|0
|0
|2
|1
|11
|–10
|[[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|NCL}}
|1979
|2011
|2
|0
|0
|2
|1
|20
|–19
|[[Oceania Football Confederation|OFC]]
|- bgcolor=#dfd
|{{fb|MNP}}
|2007
|2024
|15
|11
|2
|2
|48
|18
|30
|[[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|OMA}}
|2015
|2016
|2
|0
|1
|1
|0
|1
|–1
|[[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|PAK}}
|2008
|2008
|1
|0
|0
|1
|2
|9
|–7
|[[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|PLE}}
|2006
|2006
|1
|0
|0
|1
|0
|11
|–11
|[[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|PNG}}
|1995
|1995
|1
|0
|0
|1
|0
|9
|–9
|[[Oceania Football Confederation|OFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|PHI}}
|2000
|2026
|6
|0
|0
|6
|2
|18
|–16
|[[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|PUR}}
|2026
|2026
|1
|0
|0
|1
|0
|4
|–4
|[[CONCACAF]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|SGP}}
|2015
|2023
|3
|0
|1
|2
|3
|5
|–2
|[[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|SOL}}
|1975
|2011
|3
|0
|0
|3
|2
|24
|–22
|[[Oceania Football Confederation|OFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|SRI}}
|2008
|2008
|1
|0
|0
|1
|1
|5
|–4
|[[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|SYR}}
|2019
|2021
|2
|0
|0
|2
|0
|7
|–7
|[[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|TAH}}
|1993
|1993
|1
|0
|0
|1
|0
|11
|–11
|[[Oceania Football Confederation|OFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|TJK}}
|2000
|2000
|1
|0
|0
|1
|0
|16
|–16
|[[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]]
|- bgcolor=#ffd
|{{fb|TKM}}
|2015
|2015
|2
|1
|0
|1
|1
|1
|0
|[[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|VAN}}
|1995
|2011
|2
|0
|0
|2
|0
|2
|–2
|[[Oceania Football Confederation|OFC]]
|- bgcolor=#fdd
|{{fb|VIE}}
|1996
|2000
|2
|0
|0
|2
|0
|2
|–20
|[[Asia Football Confederation|AFC]]
|}

== Honours ==
===Regional===
* '''[[Football at the Micronesian Games|Micronesian Games]]'''
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (1): 1998

===Friendly===
* '''[[Marianas Cup]] (3)''': 2007, 2008, 2010

* '''[[FIFA Series]]'''
**{{bronze3}} '''3rd Place (1):''' [[2026 FIFA Series (men's matches)#Puerto Rico|2026]]<ref name="2026 FIFA Series champions" />

==References==
{{Reflist}}

==External links==
{{Commons category-inline}}
* {{official website|http://www.guamfa.com/}} {{in lang|en|es}}
* [https://www.the-afc.com/en/east/guam.html Guam] at [[Asian Football Confederation|AFC]]
* [https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/associations/GUM Guam] at [[FIFA]]

{{Football in Guam}}
{{EAFF teams}}
{{AFC teams}}
{{National sports teams of Guam}}

[[Category:Guam national football team| ]]
[[Category:Guam men's national football teams| ]]
[[Category:Asian national association football teams]]
[[Category:1975 establishments in Guam]]
